userdoc.notice: Constants is a library for storing constants. Simple! Don't put everything in here, just         the stuff used in multiple contracts. Constants that only apply to a single contract         should be defined in that contract instead.

documentation.text:
@notice Special address to be used as the tx origin for gas estimation calls in the
         OptimismPortal and CrossDomainMessenger calls. You only need to use this address if
         the minimum gas limit specified by the user is not actually enough to execute the
         given message and you're attempting to estimate the actual necessary gas limit. We
         use address(1) because it's the ecrecover precompile and therefore guaranteed to
         never have any code on any EVM chain.

@notice The storage slot that holds the address of a proxy implementation.
 @dev `bytes32(uint256(keccak256('eip1967.proxy.implementation')) - 1)`

@notice The storage slot that holds the address of the owner.
 @dev `bytes32(uint256(keccak256('eip1967.proxy.admin')) - 1)`
@notice The address that represents ether when dealing with ERC20 token addresses.

@notice The address that represents the system caller responsible for L1 attributes
         transactions.

@notice Returns the default values for the ResourceConfig. These are the recommended values
         for a production network.
